text.title: LLM Classifier Design Failure Modes
text.summary: Five recurring failure modes when designing LLM classifiers.

antipattern.description: Chat-shape per item — one API call per item with conversation context.
antipattern.symptom: Cost 50-100x what batched single tool call would be; latency too.
antipattern.root-cause: Default to chat shape for any LLM task.
antipattern.fix: Single forced tool call + batch (rules r1, r3).

antipattern.description: Largest model by default ("opus is best").
antipattern.symptom: Bill 20x what flash/haiku would have produced at same κ.
antipattern.fix: Calibrate smallest model first; only escalate if κ <0.7 (rule r2).

antipattern.description: No prompt cache on the system prefix.
antipattern.symptom: Input-token cost is full price every batch instead of cached price.
antipattern.root-cause: Cache marker not set; prefix shorter than cache threshold.
antipattern.fix: Cacheable system prefix; verify hit rate ≥80% (rule r4).

antipattern.symptom: Quality drift discovered via customer complaint, not eval.

antipattern.root-cause: Prompt asks for string output instead of forced tool call.
antipattern.fix: Forced tool call + enum schema (rules r1, r5).
